inputs: sequential coordinator inspection of pyproject.toml, README.md, contract2agent/cli.py, docs, tests, examples, scripts, and CI files
source.notes: This registry preserves historical evidence where visible. Tests were not run during the 2026-05-07 docs/harness bootstrap task.

status_policy.verified_pass_requires: Concrete evaluator, test, command, or artifact evidence tied to the feature. Historical evidence must keep its date and must not be presented as a fresh validation run.
status_policy.unknown_policy: Unknown or uninspected features must not be marked failed. Use needs_verification, inferred, implemented_pending_evaluation, or blocked with explicit notes.
status_policy.failed_policy: This registry intentionally has no failed default status. A failure claim requires evaluator/test evidence and should be recorded in progress or handoff before changing feature status.

features.name: c2a CLI and legacy agentdoctor alias
features.summary: The package exposes the primary c2a console script and a legacy agentdoctor alias that dispatch to contract2agent.cli:main.

evidence.detail: [project.scripts] declares c2a and agentdoctor.
CLI main dispatches to Typer or argparse fallback.
Records python -m pytest passing 355 tests on 2026-05-06.

risks: The legacy alias is compatibility surface; do not rename casually.
features.notes: Verified status rests on dated repository evidence, not a test run from the 2026-05-07 bootstrap task.

features.name: Requirement parsing and demo/scaffold generation
features.summary: Requirement text and contract files can be turned into generated demo or scaffold projects.

evidence.detail: Modules implement parsing, compilation, and template rendering.
Covers parser, demo generation, counterexamples, and CLI flows.
Records full pytest pass on 2026-05-06.

risks: Template changes affect generated project behavior and should use focused tests.
features.notes: Preserve legacy scaffold behavior unless a sprint contract explicitly changes it.

features.name: Contract trace validation
features.summary: Contracts can be checked against trace JSON for forbidden tools, ordering, required outputs, limits, and refusal behavior.

evidence.detail: Implements trace checking and CheckResult.
Covers pass/fail trace behavior and CLI checks.
Golden diagnosis fixtures exercise expected failure categories.

risks: Weakening checker rules can change many diagnosis and golden outcomes.
features.notes: Historical full-suite pass recorded on 2026-05-06.

features.name: Trace diagnosis, why reports, and regression trace suggestions
features.summary: Diagnosis flows produce structured issues, Markdown/YAML reports, why explanations, and suggested regression traces.

evidence.detail: Implements diagnosis schema, reports, why output, and regression trace helpers.
Covers diagnosis issue protocol and report rendering.
Covers Markdown/JSON report structure.

risks: Large legacy modules need careful scoped edits.
features.notes: Verified status relies on dated test evidence, not this docs-only task.

features.name: Quick, deep, and auto diagnostic modes
features.summary: Diagnostic modes run bounded smoke, multi-round, and auto workflows with review policies and confidence estimates.

evidence.detail: Implements quick, deep, auto, review policies, safety checks, and reports.
Covers mode behavior, clean errors, auto safety, and rollback.

risks: Auto mode safety boundaries and write allowlists are critical.
features.notes: Do not expand auto behavior without a sprint contract and focused tests.

features.name: Diagnostic baseline snapshots and comparison
features.summary: Diagnostic runs can save baselines, hashes, snapshots, and comparisons for regression context.

evidence.detail: Implements baseline save, load, hash, and comparison.
Covers snapshots, comparisons, corrupt baselines, and CLI parsing.

risks: Runtime .agentdoctor paths are compatibility storage paths, not project identity.
features.notes: Historical full-suite evidence recorded on 2026-05-06.

features.name: Static triage and recommended next mode
features.summary: Static triage detects configuration, risks, coverage gaps, baseline state, and recommended next command.

evidence.detail: Implements static discovery, classification, coverage, risk, planning, and reports.
Covers triage CLI, path safety, risk mapping, recommendations, and secret exclusion.

risks: Triage is static and must not be described as running agents or tools.
features.notes: No fresh triage command was run in this bootstrap task.

features.name: Static diagnostic time and cost estimate
features.summary: Cost estimate reports model likely diagnostic cost, time, tool calls, review burden, and guardrails without live execution.

evidence.detail: Implements static estimate models, rules, guardrails, and reports.
Covers estimates, budgets, guardrails, reports, and secret exclusion.

risks: Do not report estimates as measured runtime.
features.notes: Estimate behavior is static by design.

features.name: Preview-only patch proposals
features.summary: Patch preview groups findings and writes proposal reports and diffs without applying patches by default.

evidence.detail: Implements preview models, strategy routing, allowlists, reports, and diffs.
Covers preview-only behavior, allowlists, safety, output files, and no auto-apply marking.

risks: Never imply patch-preview directly applies changes unless future implementation and tests add it.
features.notes: Preview-only boundary is intentional.

features.name: Generalized agent profile evaluation
features.summary: Agent profiles are classified, matched to eval categories, scored preliminarily, and rendered with evidence basis and limitations.

evidence.detail: Implements schemas, classification, evidence resolution, scoring, prediction, and reports.
Covers schemas, classification invariants, evidence, scoring, prediction, benchmark discipline, and reports.

risks: Declared capability alone must not become high-confidence performance evidence.
features.notes: Broad pre-runtime evaluation is not a universal judge.

features.name: Benchmark and reference evidence discipline
features.summary: Benchmark and methodology references are contextual unless a comparable observed or imported result is linked.

evidence.detail: Separates observed, imported, reference, declared-only, and missing evidence.
Covers benchmark references not becoming direct scores.
Static reference metadata used for context.

risks: Do not turn public benchmark references into target-agent scores.
features.notes: No external web research was performed in this bootstrap task.

features.name: c2a eval-agent Markdown and JSON reports
features.summary: The eval-agent command renders profile-based evaluation reports in Markdown or JSON using optional results and benchmark references.

evidence.detail: Implements _cmd_eval_agent and CLI registration.
Contains sample profiles, benchmark references, and experiment results.
Includes eval-agent CLI smoke coverage.

risks: Output must keep limitations and missing evidence visible.
features.notes: No eval-agent command was run in the 2026-05-07 bootstrap task.

features.name: File-reading corpus import and task validation
features.summary: File-reading eval can import local corpora, build/load task JSONL, and validate evidence spans and file boundaries.

evidence.detail: Implements corpus import, manifest creation, task loading, and validation.
Covers corpus, task, span, and forbidden-file validation.

risks: Corpus import must not read secrets or escape approved roots.
features.notes: File-reading modules are present, so file-reading eval architecture rules apply.

features.name: Black-box file-reading agent runner
features.summary: The file-eval runner executes a target command with task input/output placeholders and captures run artifacts.

evidence.detail: Implements black-box target command runs and artifact capture.
Contains dummy readers for local smoke and docs examples.
Covers dummy target agent run and task budget behavior.

risks: Target commands are local runtime behavior and must stay bounded by explicit inputs and budgets.
features.notes: No file-eval run was executed in the 2026-05-07 bootstrap task.

features.name: Deterministic file-reading graders
features.summary: Deterministic graders evaluate answer correctness, citation grounding, file selection, abstention, schema compliance, and forbidden-file behavior.

evidence.detail: Implements deterministic grading.
Covers citation spans, recall/precision, forbidden files, abstention, and output schema validation.

risks: Optional judges must not replace deterministic scores.
features.notes: Default file-reading grading is local and API-free.

features.name: File-reading profile and run reports
features.summary: File-reading reports render profile-only readiness or observed run results while keeping limitations explicit.

evidence.detail: Implements Markdown/JSON report rendering.
Covers run reports and profile-only no-observed-score message.

risks: Profile-only reports must not claim observed reading performance.
features.notes: Observed performance requires a documented file-eval run artifact.

features.name: File-reading reference comparison
features.summary: File-reading reference comparisons compute deltas only when task pack, scoring method, environment, and conditions are compatible.

evidence.detail: Implements reference metadata and compatibility-aware comparison.
Covers contextual-only incompatible references.

risks: Incompatible public benchmark references must stay contextual.
features.notes: No public benchmark claim is added by this registry entry.

features.name: Optional LLM and command judge support
features.summary: Optional judge support can supplement deterministic file-reading grades when explicitly configured, budgeted, and separated from deterministic scores.

evidence.detail: Implements command and OpenAI-compatible judge support, budgets, cache, and redaction.
Covers explicit opt-in, no-API deterministic mode, key handling, cache, compact inputs, and sanitization.

risks: API keys must never be written to reports, caches, browser code, or committed config.
features.notes: No LLM API call was made in this bootstrap task.

features.name: c2a file-eval help, doctor, plain/json/no-color modes
features.summary: File-eval CLI includes workflow help topics, doctor checks, and script-friendly output modes.

evidence.detail: Registers file-eval commands and help topics.
Covers help topics, doctor JSON/plain modes, and output behavior.

risks: CLI docs must match actual command names.
features.notes: Codemap now lists the source-implemented profile-only command name.

features.name: Static browser agent-eval demo
features.summary: The agent-eval GitHub Pages demo is static, browser-local, bilingual, and exports Markdown/JSON without backend calls.

evidence.detail: Static browser demo files are present.
Covers static demo files, inputs, i18n, sample loading, source references, and static constraints.

risks: No backend, API calls, private keys, live benchmark fetches, or arbitrary experiment runners on GitHub Pages.
features.notes: No browser-run tests were added or run in this task.

features.name: Static contract-dispute playground
features.summary: The legacy browser-side contract-dispute playground remains available as a static specialized demo.

evidence.detail: Legacy static demo files are present.
Covers playground assets, structured output, exports, and case-specific static behavior.

risks: Legacy demo should remain static and not become the main project identity.
features.notes: Preserved as a legacy specialized demo.

features.name: File-reading examples and docs alignment
features.summary: Committed file-reading examples, docs, sample outputs, expected reports, and fixture agents are checked for consistency and safety.

evidence.detail: Contains corpus, tasks, dummy agents, target outputs, profiles, references, and expected report shapes.
Covers docs/examples alignment, safety, sample run walkthrough, and deterministic grader fixtures.

risks: Example reports are sample/synthetic unless tied to a documented run.
features.notes: Historical test evidence exists; this task did not rerun docs example tests.

features.name: Repo-local agent-first development harness
features.summary: Harness docs, progress, gates, eval matrix, sprint template, runbook, and registry coordinate future scoped agent work.

evidence.detail: Harness docs and registry files are present and updated for the bootstrap task.
Records harness doctor, docs validation, docs links, MkDocs, compileall, and pytest passing on 2026-05-06.
The 2026-05-07 bootstrap task did not run harness validator, docs link check, MkDocs, or pytest.

risks: Current docs changed after historical validation; rerun relevant gates before restoring verified_pass.
Pre-existing forbidden dirty files remain outside this task scope.
features.notes: Downgraded from historical verified_pass to implemented_pending_evaluation because this task updates harness docs but only validates JSON.

features.name: Project-scoped Codex agent configuration
features.summary: Project-local Codex configuration and role files define intended read-only and write-scoped agent behavior.

evidence.detail: Configuration files are present in the worktree.
Runtime support should be verified before marking this feature verified.

risks: Runtime behavior depends on Codex support and was not verified.
features.notes: Keep needs_verification until an actual Codex workflow or static validation evidence is recorded.

features.name: Deterministic counterexample trace generation
features.summary: Counterexample commands generate deterministic trace cases and manifests for contract validation.

evidence.detail: Implements CounterexampleCase and generation logic.
Contains counterexample tests, but this registry row is split out from a broader verified feature.

risks: Split-out feature row has not been independently evaluated as a registry item.
features.notes: Defaulting to needs_verification for a newly split-out feature candidate.

features.name: Restrictions and capability evidence reporting
features.summary: CLI commands summarize forbidden restrictions and capability evidence from contracts and optional eval reports.

evidence.detail: Implements capability report and restrictions CLI handling.
Contains capability command tests, but this row is newly split out.

risks: Capability evidence status must not be upgraded without evaluator evidence.
features.notes: Needs focused verification before verified_pass as a separate feature.

features.name: Path containment, secret filtering, and generated-artifact exclusions
features.summary: Safety helpers and subsystem checks prevent path escapes, secret leakage, and accidental generated artifact inclusion.

evidence.detail: Provides path containment helpers.
Tests reference sanitization, secret exclusion, and path allowlists.

risks: This is a cross-cutting safety boundary and should not be weakened.
features.notes: Tracked as an internal capability; use focused evidence before promoting to verified_pass.

features.name: CI and docs deployment workflows
features.summary: GitHub Actions workflows exist for pytest/JS syntax/whitespace checks and MkDocs Pages deployment.

evidence.detail: Workflows define CI and docs deployment jobs.
No current GitHub Actions run status was inspected in this task.

risks: Workflow config presence is not proof of current green CI.
features.notes: Keep needs_verification until a current CI result is checked.
